title: 6 Roles Tom Holland Bombed, Lost, Or Bailed On Before He Became Spider-Man
dek: Before he was swinging through Queens, Tom Holland was getting rejected from Star Wars, losing parts to Timothée Chalamet, and giggling his way out of a galaxy far, far away.

items.heading: Spider-Man (MCU) — The One He Actually Won, After a Brutal 5-Month Gauntlet
items.text: Tom Holland beat out thousands of hopefuls for Peter Parker, with casting director Sarah Finn later revealing they read 2,500 actors before landing on him. Holland has said the process took five months and included eight auditions, five self-tapes, and screen tests opposite Robert Downey Jr. and Chris Evans before he finally got the part.

images.caption: Tom Holland, the actor who beat 2,500 others for Spider-Man

items.heading: Star Wars: The Force Awakens — He Couldn't Stop Laughing at a Fake Droid
items.text: Around the same time he was chasing Spider-Man, Holland auditioned for what he believed was John Boyega's eventual role of Finn in the Star Wars sequel trilogy. He's admitted the audition fell apart because he couldn't keep a straight face while reading opposite a woman making robotic 'bleep bloop' noises as a stand-in droid, and he didn't land the part.

images.caption: John Boyega, who ultimately played Finn

items.heading: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle — He Was Actually Cast, Then Had to Drop Out
items.text: Holland was originally set to play Jefferson 'Seaplane' McDonough in Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle, but he had to bow out once his Spider-Man commitments took over his schedule. Nick Jonas stepped in and ended up playing the role instead.

images.caption: Nick Jonas, who replaced Holland as Seaplane McDonough

items.heading: Wonka — He Made the Final Two, Then Lost to His Girlfriend's Co-Star
items.text: Years after Spider-Man, Holland and Timothée Chalamet reportedly came down to the final two contenders for the title role in Wonka, alongside names like Donald Glover, Ezra Miller, and Ryan Gosling. Director Paul King ultimately went with Chalamet and offered him the part without even auditioning him.

images.caption: Timothée Chalamet, who landed the title role in Wonka

items.heading: November Criminals — He Read for the Lead Opposite Chloë Grace Moretz
items.text: Holland auditioned to play Addison Schacht, the grieving teen investigating his friend's murder in the crime drama November Criminals, and was reportedly devastated when he didn't land it. The role instead went to Ansel Elgort, who starred opposite Chloë Grace Moretz in the 2017 film.

images.caption: Ansel Elgort, who played Addison Schacht instead

items.heading: Peaky Blinders — The Mystery Rejection Even the Creator Wants to Fix
items.text: Holland has confirmed he auditioned for the hit BBC gangster series Peaky Blinders and didn't get cast, though he's never revealed which role he was up for. When series creator Steven Knight heard about the rejection, he speculated it must have been a major part and publicly invited Holland to join the long-gestating Peaky Blinders movie instead.

images.caption: Tom Holland, still waiting for his Peaky Blinders redemption arc
